because.text: P1-A: verifyDir silently drops any record whose body fails canonicalization (if err == nil), so a store Python rejects verifies clean in Go (0 records, 0 errors, exit 0). Violates the design rule: two implementations MUST agree on every verification outcome. P1-B: Go verify walks a flat *.warrant.json dir and cannot verify the .warrants/{records,blobs} store layout the reference impl and README define; the records/<wid>.json filename claim is never checked against recomputation. Also missing: the cross-impl CI diff step from issue #1. Canonicalization itself is byte-exact on adversarial content (HTML chars, backspace/formfeed, controls, unicode) - differential-tested, solid work.
